extracted_information: The article details horizontal scaling for WebSockets, addressing challenges like state management and broadcasting. It contrasts vertical and horizontal scaling, highlighting the stateful nature of WebSocket connections compared to stateless REST APIs. For load balancing, it recommends using sticky sessions with tools like HAProxy to ensure a user's requests are consistently routed to the same WebSocket instance. For broadcasting messages across multiple WebSocket instances, it proposes a Pub/Sub (Publish/Subscribe) pattern, specifically using Redis as a messaging system.

scaling_types.vertical_scaling: Adding more resources (CPU, memory, IO) to a single instance. Easiest but limited by hardware and non-linear performance gains.
scaling_types.horizontal_scaling: Adding more instances. Allows nearly infinite scaling, supports dynamic scaling, requires load balancer and potentially additional services (e.g., messaging).

strategies.default_haproxy_strategy: Round-robin (each request forwarded to next backend iteratively)
strategies.recommended_websocket_strategy: Leastconn (new user connected to instance with lowest overall number of connections)

load_balancing.health_checks: Ensures requests are not forwarded to inactive backends.

redis_implementation_details.client_separation: Separate clients for subscriber and publisher (subscriber client cannot publish commands)
redis_implementation_details.client_duplication: Use 'duplicate' method to create a copy of a Redis client for subscriber/publisher roles
redis_implementation_details.message_handling: Subscribe to 'message' event to receive published messages, then publish messages to specific channels.

features.name: Sticky Sessions (Sticky Connection)
features.description: Ensures that all requests from a specific user are forwarded to the particular WebSocket instance they initially connected to. Implemented by configuring the load balancer (e.g., HAProxy) to use 'leastconn' balancing and signing each user's request with a cookie containing the backend name.

features.name: Pub/Sub for Broadcasting
features.description: Enables communication between different WebSocket instances to ensure messages are broadcasted to all connected clients, regardless of which instance they are connected to. Instances subscribe to a shared channel (e.g., in Redis), and when a message is published, all instances receive it and forward it to their respective clients.
